derivatives.name: indpro
derivatives.definition: The raw Federal Reserve Industrial Production Index — a monthly measure of real output from US manufacturing, mining, and utilities, indexed to 2017=100.
derivatives.formula: raw column (FRED: INDPRO, monthly frequency, seasonally adjusted)
derivatives.notes: Non-stationary in levels. Unit: index. COVID-19 April 2020 outlier (-12.7% MoM) is a known extreme observation.

derivatives.name: indpro_yoy
derivatives.definition: The year-over-year percentage change in industrial production — how much output has grown or contracted compared with the same month last year.
derivatives.notes: Tournament signal code S2_yoy. Approximately stationary.

derivatives.name: indpro_mom
derivatives.definition: The month-over-month percentage change in industrial production — the near-term momentum of the manufacturing and mining sector.
derivatives.notes: Tournament signal code S3_mom. Approximately stationary.

derivatives.name: indpro_zscore
derivatives.definition: The 36-month rolling z-score of industrial production — how far the current IP level deviates from its recent 3-year mean, in standard deviation units.
derivatives.notes: Tournament signal code S4_zscore. Stationary by construction.

derivatives.name: indpro_3m_ma
derivatives.definition: The 3-month moving average of industrial production — a smoothed version that removes high-frequency noise from the monthly series.
derivatives.notes: Tournament signal code S5_3m_ma. Non-stationary in levels.

derivatives.name: indpro_6m_ma
derivatives.definition: The 6-month moving average of industrial production — a slower-moving trend filter than the 3-month average, capturing half-year industrial cycles.
derivatives.notes: Tournament signal code S6_6m_ma. Non-stationary in levels; used for trend deviation diagnostics.

derivatives.name: indpro_accel
derivatives.definition: The acceleration of industrial production — the change in the month-over-month rate of change, capturing whether the IP growth rate is itself speeding up or slowing down.
derivatives.notes: WINNER signal (tournament code S8_accel). Used with T2_roll_p75 threshold (gt rule). Stationary by construction as a second-difference of levels.

derivatives.name: xlp
derivatives.definition: The raw adjusted close price of the XLP ETF (Consumer Staples Select Sector SPDR). All target-axis derivatives are computed from this single series.
derivatives.formula: raw column (Yahoo Finance, auto_adjust=True, monthly last close)
derivatives.notes: Adjusted for dividends and splits. Sample starts 1998 (shorter than INDPRO which begins 1919).

derivatives.name: xlp_ret_1m
derivatives.definition: The 1-month simple return of XLP — how much the ETF gained or lost in the current month.
derivatives.notes: Base return used in tournament performance calculations.

derivatives.name: xlp_ret_3m
derivatives.definition: The 3-month total return of XLP — cumulative price change over a quarter.

derivatives.name: xlp_ret_12m
derivatives.definition: The 12-month total return of XLP — one year of cumulative price appreciation, a long-horizon trend measure.

derivatives.name: xlp_vol_12m
derivatives.definition: The 12-month rolling realized volatility of XLP monthly returns, annualized — how volatile the ETF has been over the past year.
derivatives.notes: Risk metric for regime characterization. Decimal output (0.12 = 12% annualized).

notes: Wave 10B retro-apply: migrated from flat-array structure to schema-compliant axis_block format per QA-CL BLOCK. Controls (VIX, yield spread, UNRATE, CAPUT) remain out of scope per ECON-SD.
